Children.Text:
INTERVIEWER READ OUT: Hello, my name is ___________________________________ and I work for %name_of_survey_firm%. The International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) also known as the World Bank has commissioned %name_of_survey_firm% to carry out Childcare Situation Assessment Demand-side Survey in %name_of_country%. The purpose of this study is to understand childcare arrangements and needs in %name_of_country%.

We would like to invite you to participate in this survey. If you agree to participate in this survey, we will ask you questions related to childcare arrangements in your household. The interview should last about 2 hours. We ask you to be as honest and open as possible.

Your participation in this survey is completely voluntary and you are free to choose whether to be in it or not. There will be no penalty to you or loss of benefits to which you are otherwise entitled. If you agree to this interview, you can still choose not to answer any question during the interview, or to terminate the interview at any time.

Your survey data will be handled as confidentially as possible. The information collected will be only shared with the World Bank for research and statistical purposes. If results of this survey are published or presented, individual names, and other personally identifiable information will not be used and no identifying information will be shared with any third party. Your responses will be numbered, and the code linking your name with your responses will also be stored in encrypted files on password-protected computers. We will retain these records for up to 2 years after the survey is over.

If you have any questions about this survey or about the processing of your personal data, you can ask us now or call %survey_phone_number% (%survey_contact_person%).

Do you agree to participate?

ValidationConditions.Expression:
/* 

Objective: flag if a female head of household has more than one husband.

Logic:
1. Identify the head/reference person (s01q03 == 1) who is female (s01q02 == 2).
2. Count how many spouses (s01q03 == 2) exist in the household.
3. Return false if the count exceeds 1 (unlikely scenario), otherwise true.
*/

// If the member is the female head of household
s01q03 == 1 && s01q02 == 2 ?

// Check if there is more than one spouse in the household
members.Count(member => member.s01q03 == 2) <= 1 :

true

ValidationConditions.Message: Age %self% seems to be unlikely high. Please confirm that this is correct.
The age of the reference person cannot be %self% please check.

ValidationConditions.Expression:
/* 
Household head/reference person must be at least 14 years older than their children
- s01q03: relationship to reference person (1=Self, 3=Child)
- s01q04_years: age in years
- members: roster of household members
*/

// if member is head/reference (relationship == 1)
s01q03 == 1 ? !members.Any(x => x.s01q03 == 3 && (s01q04_years - x.s01q04_years) < 14) :

// if member is child (relationship == 3)
s01q03 == 3 ? !members.Any(x => x.s01q03 == 1 && (x.s01q04_years - s01q04_years) < 14) :

true
ValidationConditions.Message: The reference person must be at least 14 years older than his/her children. Please correct either the [relationship to the reference person](s01q02) or the [age](s01q04_years)
The reference person must be at least 14 years younger than his/her parents. Please correct either the [relationship to the reference person](s01q02) or the [age](s01q04_years)

ValidationConditions.Expression:
/* 
reference person must be at least 28 years younger than their grandparents

Variables:
- s01q03 = relationship to head (1 = SELF)
- s01q04_years = age in years
- members = roster of household members
- relationship codes: 1 = head, 4 = grandparent
*/

// if current member is head (SELF), no grandparent with age difference less than 28 years
s01q03 == 1 ?
    !members.Any(x => x.s01q03 == 4 && (s01q04_years - x.s01q04_years) < 28) :

// if current member is grandparent, no reference person with age diffference less than 28 years
s01q03 == 4 ?
    !members.Any(x => x.s01q03 == 1 && (x.s01q04_years - s01q04_years) < 28) :

true
ValidationConditions.Message: The reference person must be at least 28 years younger than his/her grandparents. Please correct either the [relationship to the reference person](s01q02) or the [age](s01q04_years)
The reference person must be at least 15 years old. Please correct or explain.

Children.Label: Random number between 1 and 5
Children.Name: s07a_random_number
Children.Expression:
/*
@title
Generate a random number corresponding to the selected vignette

@description
Creates a random number based on:

- Seconds component of the member's module timestamp
- Rowcode of the member

@details
For lack of the ability to create a random number for N members in M modules,
this function relies on the practically random value of the seconds component
of the timestamp to create a random number.

To combat similar random numbers across rows, the function also uses the
rowcode as a person-specific component in the calculation.

To prevent clustering of selections, prime numbers, which are not factors
of any other numbers, are:

- Multplied by each component to scale
- Added as an offset

To return a number a number ranging from 1 to 5:

- take the modulo operator of the sum of components, yielding values [0,5)
- take the floor, yielding values [0,4]
- shift the resulting value by 1, yielding the desired range of [1,5]

*/
((s07a_module_start.Value.Second * 31 + @rowcode * 43 + 7) % 5) + 1

Children.Text: INTERVIEWER READ: "We will start with telling you a short, hypothetical story about a couple in [COUNTRY]. %s07a_13a_woman_name% is a woman whom you don't know. She lives in a neighborhood like yours with her husband, %s07a_13a_man_name%, who has steady work as a wage laborer and earns enough for the family to get by. She also has a seasonal job working in the fields during harvest season, which lasts 4 months. She has a child who is 12 months old. A new free daycare center opened near the field where she works, and it offers a clean and safe environment for children. A daycare center is a place that offers facilities to care for multiple children, often with structured activities and a licensed or qualified staff."

Children.Title: Section 7A: Social Norms - Vignette Experiment

Children.Text: INTERVIEWER: THE RESPONDENT FOR THIS MODULE SHOULD BE A MOTHER.

Children.QuestionText: 1. INTERVIEWER: IS <font color="purple">%rostertitle%</font> RESPONDING FOR HERSELF?

Children.Text: INTERVIEWER: PLEASE DOUBLE CHECK TO ENSURE THAT YOU HAVE SOUGHT TO INTERVIEW THE INDIVIDUAL IN PRIVATE OR WHERE OTHER MEMBERS OF THE HOUSEHOLD CANNOT OVERHEAR OR CONTRIBUTE ANSWERS.

Children.QuestionText: 2. INTERVIEWER:WHO IS PRESENT DURING THE INTERVIEW OF <font color="purple">%rostertitle%</font>?

Children.Text:
3. I will read out a series of statements to you now. For each of the statements, indicate your level of agreement or disagreement.

INTERVIEWER: READ OUT EACH STATEMENT AND RECORD THE RESPONSE.

Children.QuestionText: a. Only the mother can provide a young child with the care they need. When I say young child, I mean a child aged 6 years or younger. Please keep this in mind as you answer the questions in the rest of the module.
b. Young children are only safe with their mothers or direct family like older siblings or grandparents.
c. Attending childcare settings (e.g. daycare/ nursery/preschool/childminder) helps young children develop social and learning skills
d. Families do not need to be concerned about a child's learning before the age of 3.
4. At what age is it appropriate to let someone other than the mother or father take care of the child for several hours a day?

5. Now think about members of your community. These could be other mothers you know, your neighbors or friends, your in-laws, etc. At what age do they think it is appropriate to let someone other than the mother, father or close family member take care of the child for several hours a day?

Children.Text: I'm going to read you a statement and will ask you about your own beliefs, and then about what members of your community think and do.

Children.QuestionText: 6. Could you tell us whether you agree, disagree, or neither agree nor disagree that when a mother works for pay, the children suffer?

Children.Instructions: INTERVIEWER:DON'T READ THE STATEMENT UNLESS REQUESTED BY RESPONDENT: WHEN A MOTHER WORKS FOR PAY, THE CHILDREN SUFFER
Children.QuestionText: 7. Now think about members of your community. How many of them would agree with the same statement?
8. How many mothers with young children in your community work for pay?
9. How will people in your community react if a mother with young children works outside the home for pay?

10. Back to your beliefs.

Do you agree, disagree, or neither agree nor disagree that a young child should mainly be taken care of by the mother throughout the day?

Children.Instructions: INTERVIEWER:DON'T READ THE STATEMENT UNLESS REQUESTED BY RESPONDENT: A YOUNG CHILD SHOULD MAINLY BE TAKEN CARE OF BY THE MOTHER THROUGHOUT THE DAY
Children.QuestionText: 11. How many members of your community would agree with the same statement?
12. How many young children in your community are taken care of mainly by their mothers throughout the day?
13. Once again, do <u>you</u> agree, disagree, or neither agree nor disagree that if the mother works, her young children should only be taken care of by family members.

Children.Instructions: INTERVIEWER:DON'T READ THE STATEMENT UNLESS REQUESTED BY RESPONDENT:  IF THE MOTHER WORKS, HER YOUNG CHILDREN SHOULD ONLY BE TAKEN CARE OF BY FAMILY MEMBERS
Children.QuestionText: 14. How many members of your community would agree with the same statement?
15. How many working mothers in your community rely on someone outside the family for childcare for their young children?

16. Back to you again. Remember we want to know about your beliefs now.

Do you agree, disagree, or neither agree nor disagree that it is okay for parents to send their young children to childcare (e.g. daycare/ nursery/preschool/childminder)?

Children.Instructions: INTERVIEWER:DON'T READ THE STATEMENT UNLESS REQUESTED BY RESPONDENT: IT IS OKAY FOR PARENTS TO SEND THEIR YOUNG CHILDREN TO CHILDCARE (E.G. DAYCARE/ NURSERY/PRESCHOOL/CHILDMINDER)
Children.QuestionText: 17. How many members of your community would agree with the same statement?
18. How many parents in your community send their young children to childcare (e.g. daycare/ nursery/preschool/childminder)?

Children.Label: Random number from 1 to 5
Children.Name: v1_random_number
Children.Expression:
/*
@title
Generate a random number corresponding to the selected vignette

@description
Creates a random number based on:

- Seconds component of the member's module timestamp
- Rowcode of the member

@details
For lack of the ability to create a random number for N members in M modules,
this function relies on the practically random value of the seconds component
of the timestamp to create a random number.

To combat similar random numbers across rows, the function also uses the
rowcode as a person-specific component in the calculation.

To prevent clustering of selections, prime numbers, which are not factors
of any other numbers, are:

- Multplied by each component to scale
- Added as an offset

To return a number a number ranging from 1 to 5:

- take the modulo operator of the sum of components, yielding values [0,5)
- take the floor, yielding values [0,4]
- shift the resulting value by 1, yielding the desired range of [1,5]

*/
((sca_module_start.Value.Second * 61 + @rowcode * 73 + 13) % 5) + 1
